coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Listed across calls with senior analyst titles (SVP / Managing Director / Director at Citi); multiple consecutive turns and management-directed recognition indicate a senior, repeat coverage role. Management treats him as a go-to leisure/cruise questioner (appears repeatedly in top question slots across calls).
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Leisure/travel with a cruise-first focus; questions center on cruise economics (yields, APD), new product (private islands, river cruises) and capital allocation. He frames cruise results in the context of broader travel/land-based vacation competition.
rhetorical_signature: opens with "Hey, good morning" in 5 of 11 calls (e.g., 2022-Q4, 2024-Q3, 2024-Q4, 2025-Q3, 2025-Q4)
frequently prefaces follow-ups with "By way of follow-up" (2022-Q4) or 'Got it' / 'Makes a lot of sense' to pivot
tones are collegial and constructive — often closes with a short 'Thanks' or 'Got it' rather than adversarial challenge

reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Mostly multi-part questions: ≈9 of 11 turns contain two-or-more sub-questions (e.g., Europe + China in 2022-Q4; CapEx + modeling in 2024-Q4). He routinely asks sequential follow-ups rather than adversarial pushback; pushes deeper when answers are high-level.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He requests numeric decompositions and explicit guidance (≈5 of 11 turns ask for quantification or like-for-like decompositions — e.g., asks about '$5 billion' CapEx modeling in 2024-Q4 and 'north of 2%' contribution in 2025-Q4). He rewards specific $ / % / ship-count detail and runway mechanisms.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He anchors to management-delivered numbers and recent baselines: references the '$5 billion' CapEx guide (2024-Q4), 'pre-COVID' / 20% baseline for land-vacation gap (2022-Q4), and 'negative ship timing' from the prior year when modeling 2026 (2025-Q3). He will also cite peer/competitive moves when framing Caribbean supply (2025-Q3).

core_topics.what_they_press_on: Asks why return profile has materially improved and where long-term returns will settle; probes non-financial factors and tradeoffs (debt, buybacks, dividends) when management discusses returns. He revisits this across 2024-Q3 and 2025-Q4 when capital decisions and inorganic/organic questions arise.

Seeks clarity on Europe / China demand disruptions and the timing/impact of reopenings on yields and sourcing. He asks for quantification of any 'drag' from regions and whether reopening creates upside (2022-Q4, 2025-Q3/2025-Q4).

Requests decomposition of yield/APD growth between new hardware/inorganic items and like‑for‑like improvements; presses management for how much of next-year upside is organic. Appears in 2022-Q4, 2025-Q3 and 2025-Q4.

Asks how to model the $5bn CapEx guide and the shape of new-ship deliveries (ramp timing, maintenance vs development spending). He requests explicit year-to-year moves and how non-new-build spend will evolve (2024-Q4, 2025-Q4).

Drills into product details (river cruise ramp, passenger mix, whether kids allowed) and the economics/scale of private destinations. Wants operational shape and customer sourcing color (2024-Q4, 2024-Q3).

Pushes on leverage priorities vs opportunistic buybacks/dividends and how refinancing improvement factors into capital allocation. Asks for where balance-sheet sits relative to investment-grade metrics (2024-Q3, 2024-Q4).

recurring_concerns.blind_spots: No evidence in history of him asking about fuel hedging, sustainability / emissions strategy, or crew labor/operational costs (n_turns = 0 for these topics). Peers sometimes surface these; he has not. This is n_turns < 2; insufficient to characterise as an active concern.
recurring_concerns.stance_drift: Early in the history (2022-Q4) he emphasized regional reopening and the 'land-based vacation' gap; by 2024-Q3/4 his focus shifted to returns and capital allocation (see 2024-Q3 returns questions). By 2025-Q3/4 he concentrated more on yield decomposition and organic vs inorganic drivers for 2026 (compare 2022-Q4 → 2024-Q3 and 2024-Q4 → 2025-Q4).

queue_behavior.typical_position: #3–#10 range; breakdown: #3 in 3 of 11 calls (2024-Q4 twice and 2025-Q4), #6 in 2 calls (2022-Q4, 2025-Q3), #7 in 2 calls (2022-Q4 twice), #9 in 1 call (2024-Q3), #10 in 2 calls (2024-Q3).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early (#2–#3), he commonly opens on new product or capital questions (e.g., river cruise ramp and product details when #2 in 2024-Q4; CapEx modeling when early in 2024-Q4).

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Yes — when high-level answers are given he drills further (2024-Q3: after returns commentary he asks 'any non-financial considerations' and leverage tradeoffs).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reactive_followup: Yes — he explicitly signals follow-ups: 'By way of follow-up' (2022-Q4) and often follows earlier answers with deeper sub-questions.
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.cross_narrative_reconcile: Yes — he asks management to reconcile threads like organic vs inorganic contributors to yields ('organic versus inorganic' in 2025-Q4).
supporting_evidence: 2024-Q3 quote: 'where do you think returns ultimately settle' (reframe deeper)

explicit_peer_references.example_phrases: no explicit 'following up on X' peer citations found; uses 'By way of follow-up' (2022-Q4) but not an explicit analyst name reference.

examples: "$5 billion" (anchors CapEx question to the '$5 billion' guide in 2024-Q4)
"10 ships" (repeats management's 'initial order of 10 ships' when asking about ramp in 2024-Q4)
"negative ship timing" (builds on management's prior-year timing issue when modeling 2026 in 2025-Q3)

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He has taken additional turns within the same call multiple times (took follow-up turns in 2022-Q4, 2024-Q3 and 2024-Q4 — 3 distinct calls) and uses them primarily for clarification or deeper drilling rather than to start new topics.
